etymology.description: semantic 歹(歹) (dǎi, "death; bad") + phonetic 央(央) (yāng) -> misfortune
etymology.interpretation: The character 殃 (yāng, "misfortune") combines 歹(歹) (dǎi, "death; bad") as the semantic element, indicating a negative or destructive aspect, with 央(央) (yāng) providing the phonetic element. The concept of death or badness from 歹 informs the sense of misfortune, with the phonetic 央 contributing the pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 这件事给整个家庭带来了殃(這件事給整個家庭帶來了殃)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn shì gěi zhěnggè jiātíng dàiláile yāng.
examples.english: This situation brought misfortune to the whole family.

examples.chinese: 他因为朋友的不良行为而遭殃(他因為朋友的不良行為而遭殃)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yīnwèi péngyou de bùliáng xíngwéi ér zāoyāng.
examples.english: He suffered misfortune due to his friend's misconduct.

examples.chinese: 暴风雨过后(暴風雨過後)，村子里的庄稼都遭了殃(村子裡的莊稼都遭了殃)。
examples.pinyin: Bàofēngyǔ guòhòu，cūnzi lǐ de zhuāngjia dōu zāole yāng.
examples.english: After the storm, the crops in the village suffered misfortune.
